the liver plays a vital role in the muscular system by providing energy and nutrients to muscle cells. it also helps in the breakdown of fats and the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats. the liver filters toxins and waste products from the blood, ensuring that the body remains healthy. it stores glycogen for energy and regulates the levels of various hormones in the body. the liver also produces proteins such as albumin, which helps maintain the balance of fluids in the body. it works in conjunction with the muscular system to support the body's overall function. the liver is essential for the proper functioning of the muscular system. it helps in the contraction of muscles by supplying the necessary nutrients and energy. the liver is a key organ in the muscular system, supporting the body's physical activities. the liver is also responsible for the metabolism of drugs and other substances that the body encounters. the liver is an important organ in the muscular system, contributing to the body's ability to perform physical tasks. the liver is a critical component of the muscular system, playing a key role in maintaining overall health and function.
